reasoning: The Labour Party (Ap) has been the largest party by seats in every Norwegian parliamentary election since 1927, establishing an extremely strong base rate. Despite a dip in late 2024, their popularity rebounded in 2025, buoyed by the return of Jens Stoltenberg. Recent polls leading up to August 2025 consistently place Ap in first with around 26.7% to 30.9% of the vote, safely ahead of the surging Progress Party (FrP) which sits around 20-23.6%, and the Conservative Party (Høyre) at ~15-18%. With the election just 8 days away from the forecast date, it is highly likely that Ap maintains their lead and wins the most seats. The Polymarket price of 0.79 reflects strong confidence, and combining that with consistent polling leads of 3-9 points justifies an updated probability around 0.84.
